name: Potato Farls (Irish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and halve the potatoes; place them in a large saucepan with enough water to cover.
2. Bring to boil, then simmer, covered, for 20 to 30 minutes or until fork-tender.
3. Drain well, then return to the saucepan over low heat.
4. Add butter and mash the potatoes thoroughly.
5. Stir in flour and salt.
6. Form the mixture into a ball; turn onto lightly floured surface.
7. Knead gently until smooth.
8. Divide dough into two portions.
9. Roll out one portion into an 8 inch (20 cm) circle, about 1/4 inch thick.
10. Cut into quarters and set aside.
11. Repeat with the remaining dough.
12. Heat half the vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat.
13. Cook the dough pieces in batches for 2 minutes on each side or until golden brown, adding more oil as needed.
14. Serve warm.
